claimText: The Diverse Epigenetic Epidemiology Partnership (DEEP) project is a collaborative initiative led by the University of Bristol and CSIR-CCMB.
evidenceExcerpt: DEEP is a five-year project led by the University of Bristol and CSIR-CCMB in India.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the key institutions involved in the DEEP project.
distractorHooks: Led by the World Health Organization (WHO)
Led by the Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR)
A purely domestic Indian initiative without international collaboration

claimText: The DEEP project aims to explore how genomic and environmental diversity affect disease risk.
evidenceExcerpt: DEEP aims to explore the effects of genomic and environmental diversity on disease risk.
examUse: Statement-based question on the primary objective of the DEEP project.
distractorHooks: Aims to map the entire human genome
Focuses exclusively on infectious diseases rather than genomic and environmental diversity

claimText: Epigenetic changes affect gene function without altering the underlying DNA sequence.
evidenceExcerpt: Epigenetic changes are reversible and do not change the DNA sequence, but alter how the body reads it.
examUse: Statement-based question on the fundamental mechanism of epigenetics.
distractorHooks: Epigenetics involves the mutation or alteration of the DNA sequence
Epigenetics permanently rewrites the genetic code

claimText: Epigenetic changes, which can be triggered by behaviors and environmental factors, are reversible.
evidenceExcerpt: Epigenetics studies how behaviors and environment cause reversible changes that affect gene function
examUse: Statement-based question testing the reversibility and triggers of epigenetic modifications.
distractorHooks: Epigenetic changes are permanent and irreversible
Epigenetic changes are entirely independent of environmental factors and behaviors
